panels.description: Total Redis command throughput across the selected cluster and environment, calculated from redis_commands_processed_total. Useful for understanding current workload intensity.
panels.title: Total Ops/sec

panels.description: Sum of Redis memory currently used across all selected instances. This reflects the actual memory footprint reported by Redis, not host-level memory usage.
panels.title: Total Memory Used

panels.description: Total number of currently connected Redis clients across the selected instances. Includes application, replication, and internal connections.
panels.title: Total Clients

panels.description: Overall cache efficiency for the selected cluster, calculated as hits divided by hits + misses over the last 5 minutes. Higher values indicate more requests are served from cache.
panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio (Cluster)

panels.description: Shows whether each selected Redis host is reachable by the exporter. 1 = UP, 0 = DOWN. Useful for quick health validation at host level.

targets.expr: min by (redis_host) ( redis_up{job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env", redis_host=~"$host", exporter_port=~"$xport"} )

panels.title: Redis Up

panels.description: Total number of connected clients for the currently selected scope. Useful when filtering by host or port to inspect client concentration.
panels.title: Total Clients (Selected)

panels.description: Current client count grouped by host. Helps identify which Redis hosts are handling the most active connections.
panels.title: Clients by Host

panels.description: Connected client count over time for each selected Redis instance. Useful for spotting traffic growth, connection spikes, or sudden drops.
panels.title: Connected Clients

panels.description: Number of clients currently blocked waiting on blocking operations such as BLPOP, BRPOP, XREAD BLOCK, or similar commands. Persistent non-zero values may indicate application or workload bottlenecks.
panels.title: Blocked Clients

panels.description: Cumulative number of successful key lookups since Redis start or last restart. Useful for understanding long-term cache usage volume.
panels.title: Total Hits Since Restart

panels.description: Total cache hits during the currently selected dashboard time range. Useful for comparing short-term cache activity between periods.
panels.title: Hits in selected time range

panels.description: Total cache misses during the currently selected dashboard time range. Higher values may indicate weak cache locality, missing warm-up, or poor key usage patterns.
panels.title: Misses in selected time range

panels.description: Cache hit ratio for the selected time range, based on increases in hits and misses. A fast way to judge cache effectiveness for the current period.

targets.expr:
100 *
sum(increase(redis_keyspace_hits_total{job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env"}[$__range]))
/
(
  sum(increase(redis_keyspace_hits_total{job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env"}[$__range]))
+
  sum(increase(redis_keyspace_misses_total{job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env"}[$__range]))
)

panels.title: Hit Ratio in selected time range

panels.description: Redis operations processed per second across the selected scope. Useful for identifying throughput trends, bursts, and peak load windows.
panels.title: Ops per Second

panels.description: Per-second rate of keyspace hits and misses. Helps visualize cache efficiency changes over time and correlate misses with application behavior.
panels.title: Keyspace Hits vs Misses

panels.description: Total number of GET commands executed during the selected dashboard time range. Useful for measuring read demand in the current period.
panels.title: GET Operations in Selected Range

panels.description: Total number of Redis commands processed during the selected time range. Good for comparing overall workload across periods.
panels.title: Total commands executed

panels.description: Rate of GET commands per second. Useful for tracking read-heavy traffic and identifying GET-driven spikes.
panels.title: GET Operations / sec

panels.description: Current per-second command mix grouped by Redis command name. Useful for seeing which commands dominate workload and whether traffic is read-heavy, write-heavy, or operational/internal.
panels.title: Command distribution

panels.description: Top 10 Redis commands by execution rate over the last 5 minutes. Helps identify the most frequently used commands in the selected scope.
panels.title: Top Redis Commands (ops/sec)

panels.description: Top 10 commands by average execution latency over the last 5 minutes. Calculated as total command duration divided by total command count, then converted to milliseconds.
panels.title: Top Slow Commands (avg ms)

panels.description: Number of keys currently stored per Redis database (db0, db1, etc.) across the selected scope. Useful for understanding data distribution across logical DBs.
panels.title: Keyspace Size by DB

panels.description: Current number of entries in the Redis slowlog for each selected instance. Non-zero values indicate Redis has recorded slow commands above the configured slowlog threshold.

targets.expr:
redis_slowlog_length{
  job="redis-exporters",
  redis_cluster=~"$cluster",
  environment=~"$env",
  redis_host=~"$host",
  exporter_port=~"$xport"
}

panels.title: Slowlog Length

panels.description: Execution time of the most recent slow command recorded by Redis, shown in milliseconds. Useful for spotting recent latency outliers.
panels.title: Last Slow Execution Duration

panels.description: ID of the most recent slowlog entry. If this value keeps increasing, new slow commands are being added to the slowlog.

targets.expr:
redis_slowlog_last_id{
  job="redis-exporters",
  redis_cluster=~"$cluster",
  environment=~"$env",
  redis_host=~"$host",
  exporter_port=~"$xport"
}

panels.title: Slowlog Last ID

panels.description: Cache hit ratio over the selected dashboard time range. Useful for comparing cache effectiveness across longer periods such as 1h, 12h, or 24h.
panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio (range)

panels.description: Per-second rate of cache misses across the selected scope. Useful for identifying degraded cache efficiency or traffic patterns that bypass cached data.
panels.title: Keyspace Misses (per sec)

panels.description: Percentage of total Redis operations represented by GET commands over the last 5 minutes. Useful for estimating how read-heavy the workload is.

targets.expr:
100 *
sum(rate(redis_commands_total{cmd="get", job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env"}[5m]))
/
sum(rate(redis_commands_processed_total{job="redis-exporters", redis_cluster=~"$cluster", environment=~"$env"}[5m]))

panels.title: GET % of total ops

panels.description: Total key count per Redis host. Useful for identifying uneven data distribution across the cluster.
panels.title: Key Distribution by Node (Keys)

panels.description: Compares the most loaded node’s key count to the average node key count. Values above roughly 1.3–1.5 may indicate skewed distribution, uneven sharding, or the need for rebalancing.
panels.title: Key Imbalance (max/avg)

panels.description: Estimated Redis CPU usage per instance based on Redis user CPU plus system CPU counters, converted to percentage over time. Useful for spotting hot nodes and sustained CPU pressure inside Redis processes.

targets.expr:
sum by (redis_host, exporter_port) (
  rate(redis_cpu_user_seconds_total{
    job="redis-exporters",
    redis_cluster=~"$cluster",
    environment=~"$env",
    redis_host=~"$host",
    exporter_port=~"$xport"
  }[5m])
+
  rate(redis_cpu_sys_seconds_total{
    job="redis-exporters",
    redis_cluster=~"$cluster",
    environment=~"$env",
    redis_host=~"$host",
    exporter_port=~"$xport"
  }[5m])
) *100

panels.title: Redis CPU Usage by Instance

panels.description: Redis memory currently used per instance. Useful for seeing which nodes hold the largest working set.
panels.title: Memory Used

panels.description: Ratio between Redis allocator memory and resident memory. Values notably above 1.5 may indicate fragmentation or inefficient memory reuse.
panels.title: Memory Fragmentation Ratio

panels.description: Rate of key evictions per second. Non-zero values usually mean Redis has reached its memory limit and is evicting keys according to the configured eviction policy.
panels.title: Evicted Keys (per sec)

panels.description: Rate of expired keys per second. Useful for understanding TTL-driven churn and time-based key cleanup activity.
panels.title: Expired Keys (per sec)

panels.description: Redis network traffic in and out per second across the selected scope. Useful for spotting traffic bursts and correlating network activity with workload changes.
panels.title: Network In / Out (bytes/sec)

panels.description: Persistence-related view showing AOF enabled state and age since last RDB save. Useful for checking whether persistence is active and whether snapshots are occurring as expected.
panels.title: Persistence Status

panels.description: Raw status table for the selected Redis exporter targets. Useful for troubleshooting reachability, filtering by host/port, and validating target-level status in a tabular form.
panels.title: Target Status Table
